question: Calculate the ultimate and allowable bearing capacity of a 2.5 m wide continuous strip footing founded 1.0 m below grade in sand with friction angle 34 degrees, unit weight 19 kN/m3, and zero cohesion. Use a factor of safety of 3. Show the key numbers.

question: Set up a Sobol sensitivity analysis of bearing capacity with friction angle in [28, 36] degrees, unit weight in [17, 20] kN/m3, and footing width in [1.5, 3] m. Which input contributes most to the variance?

question: Per GEC-10 (FHWA drilled shaft guidance), describe the alpha method for unit side resistance in cohesive soils and the typical range of the alpha factor.
Per GEC-11, summarize the external stability checks required for the design of an MSE retaining wall.
What is the FHWA approach for grout-to-ground bond strength in micropile design, and what are typical bond values for a Type B micropile in sand?
Search the reference library for guidance on downdrag and negative skin friction on deep foundations and summarize what you find.

Using the GEC-10 (FHWA-NHI-18-024) alpha method, determine the alpha factor for drilled shaft side resistance in a clay with undrained shear strength su = 110 kPa (take atmospheric pressure pa = 101.3 kPa), then compute the unit side resistance and the total side shear capacity for a 1.2 m diameter shaft embedded 9 m in that clay. Cite the GEC-10 equation you used.

expected.source: v5.1 worktree ground truth, 2026-06-10 (drilled_shaft.drilled_shaft_capacity; 1437 kN applies GEC-10 exclusion zones, alt 2053 kN = full-length)

question: A 3 by 3 drilled shaft group is spaced at 3 shaft diameters center-to-center in the direction of loading. Using the p-multipliers recommended by GEC-10 (FHWA-NHI-18-024), state the lead-row and trailing-row p-multipliers and estimate the average lateral group efficiency. Cite the GEC-10 table you used.

Per GEC-8 (FHWA-HIF-07-03), estimate the unit shaft resistance of a drilled displacement (DD) pile in granular soil using the NeSmith correlation for an average SPT N60 of 22, stating the equation and any limiting value. Then give the allowable shaft capacity for a 0.45 m diameter, 12 m long pile using the GEC-8 ASD factor of safety with static load testing.

Following GEC-4 (FHWA-IF-99-015) for a tieback wall in sand (phi = 34 deg, gamma = 19 kN/m3, H = 9 m), give the apparent earth pressure (AEP) diagram peak pressure for sand and the resulting total horizontal load per unit width of wall. Also state the GEC-4 anchor proof-test load and the creep-test acceptance criteria.

Using the FHWA micropile reference (FHWA-NHI-05-039), select an ultimate grout-to-ground bond strength for a Type B micropile in medium dense to dense sand, then size the bond (load transfer) length required for a 350 kN design load using a factor of safety of 2.0 and a 175 mm drill hole diameter. State the bond value and the source table.

Using NAVFAC DM7, determine the Rankine active and passive lateral earth pressure coefficients for a vertical wall with horizontal cohesionless backfill at phi = 33 deg, then compute the total active and passive thrusts on a 6 m high wall with gamma = 18.5 kN/m3 and no water table. Cite the DM7 basis for the coefficients.

expected.source: v5.1 worktree ground truth, 2026-06-10 (retaining_walls.earth_pressure_coefficient + P=0.5*K*gamma*H^2)

Then apply the SHANSEP relationship from GEC-5 (give S and m) to estimate su for the same clay at an OCR of 4.

question: Run an SRM mesh-refinement (mesh-consistency) study on a 2:1, 10 m slope [(0,0),(10,0),(30,10),(50,10)] with c' = 10 kPa, phi' = 15 deg, gamma = 18 kN/m3 and a 5 m foundation, using the mesh sequence (16x8) then (24x12) with T6 elements.
